text: Summary Top++ is a program designed to evaluate the total inclusive cross-section for top quark pair production at the Tevatron and the LHC. In fixed order approach, it works with the full next-to-next-to leading order (NNLO) precision and in the case of including soft-gluon resummation for the hadronic cross-section - with full next-to-next-to-leading logarithmic (NNLL) precision in Mellin space. All options of the program are collected into a section at the beginning of this summary and can be chosen accordingly by the user. In general, the program allows the user to choose between a) fixed order calculation of the cross-section with full next-to-next-to leading order (NNLO) accuracy in the virtual and real corrections, and b) soft-gluon resummation for the hadronic cross-section in Mellin space with full next-to-next-to-leading logarithmic (NNLL) accuracy. Also the program has a large number (29) of other very useful options. Top++ is written entirely in C++. The interface is very simple and easy to use. It is based on common sense that has been preserved as close to the physics as possible. It does not require from the user to know how to program in order to run it. Program name: Top++ Version: 2.0 Program website: http://www.alexandermitov.com/software Top++ is distributed under the GNU Public License. No warranty is given or implied. Compiler: The program has been developed and tested using GNU Compiler Collection’s C++ compiler. Operating system: Linux, Mac OS X. Top++ should also work on other unix systems but it has not been tested. Program language: C++ Memory requirements: The memory required to execute the program is less than 200 MB. External libraries: GSL - The GNU Scientiﬁc Library, LHAPDF - The Les Houches Accord pdf Interface.
explanation: The text is highly technical and reads like software documentation, with plausible details about NNLO/NNLL and Top++; however, minor phrasing quirks and a couple of factual slips (e.g., 'GNU Public License') suggest it could be human-written but lightly edited or generated.
